organism: Bordetella bronchiseptica (strain ATCC BAA-588 / NCTC 13252 / RB50) (Alcaligenes bronchisepticus)

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane ABC transporter / ATPase that energises export of CyaA
machinery_genes.quote: The gene products of cyaB and cyaD are highly similar to HlyB and HlyD, known to be necessary for the transport of HlyA across the cell envelope and for its release into the external medium.
machinery_genes.note: Glaser et al. 1988 (B. pertussis cya operon). cyaB product = HlyB homologue, i.e. the ABC transporter of the T1SS. Confirmed by Masin et al. 2015 review which states 'CyaB operates as an ABC family transporter'. Apparatus characterised in B. pertussis; cya locus conserved in B. bronchiseptica RB50 (this instance).

machinery_genes.role: periplasm-spanning, IM-anchored adaptor bridging the ABC transporter to the outer-membrane channel
machinery_genes.quote: The gene products of cyaB and cyaD are highly similar to HlyB and HlyD, known to be necessary for the transport of HlyA across the cell envelope and for its release into the external medium.
machinery_genes.note: Glaser et al. 1988. cyaD product = HlyD homologue, the membrane fusion protein (MFP) of the T1SS. Characterised in B. pertussis; transferred to B. bronchiseptica RB50 by locus conservation.

machinery_genes.role: TolC-family outer-membrane channel forming the exit duct of the T1SS
machinery_genes.quote: Domain structure of the outer membrane transporter protein CyaE of Bordetella pertussis.
machinery_genes.note: Gross 1995 (Mol Microbiol) title explicitly names CyaE as the outer membrane transporter protein of the CyaA secretion system; MeSH terms list 'Bacterial Outer Membrane Proteins'. Glaser et al. 1988 (PMID 2905265) had already shown secretion requires 'a third gene product specified by the cyaE gene', and Masin et al. 2015 (PMID 26391732) name CyaBDE collectively as the T1SS. CyaE is the TolC-family OMP per the apparatus brief vocabulary. Characterised in B. pertussis; conserved in B. bronchiseptica RB50.

notes: System = the Bordetella adenylate cyclase toxin-haemolysin (CyaA, an RTX toxin) type I secretion system, encoded by the cya operon (cyaCABDE) immediately downstream/around the cyaA toxin gene. The three core T1SS apparatus components are CyaB (ABC transporter, HlyB-like), CyaD (membrane fusion protein, HlyD-like) and CyaE (TolC-family outer-membrane channel). The founding apparatus characterisation (Glaser et al. 1988 EMBO J; Masure et al. 1990 Biochemistry) was done in B. pertussis using complementation with the E. coli hly operon; CyaE was further characterised as the outer-membrane transporter by Gross 1995; Masin et al. 2015 review names CyaBDE collectively as the T1SS. STRAIN TRANSFER: this instance is B. bronchiseptica RB50 (sys_instance_label BB0325, effector BB0324). The cya locus is conserved across the classical Bordetellae (B. pertussis / B. parapertussis / B. bronchiseptica); the apparatus gene names are transferred from the B. pertussis characterisation, noted per the separated-paper rule. DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED: the secreted substrate/effector CyaA (the RTX adenylate cyclase-haemolysin, the effector context here = BB0324) is not machinery; CyaC (the acyltransferase that palmitoylates/activates CyaA) is a maturation enzyme, not a transport apparatus component, so it is excluded from machinery (analogous to HlyC per the apparatus brief); no transcriptional regulators (e.g. BvgAS) listed. All three core components documented with naming quotes, so status COMPLETE. Lowest-confidence point: the explicit 'TolC-family' assignment for CyaE rests on the Gross 1995 title ('outer membrane transporter protein') plus review-level homology statements rather than an in-line abstract sentence, but the OMP identity is unambiguous across the literature.
